tldr: Move from Auth0's hosted CIAM to BoxyHQ's open-source SAML/SSO stack to drop MAU-based pricing and self-host enterprise auth.
why_switch: BoxyHQ is Apache-2.0 OSS — no MAU-based billing surprises at scale
Self-hosting keeps user data and audit logs inside your perimeter
Standards-based: SAML, SCIM, and OIDC stay portable across vendors
Auth0's enterprise feature gating disappears when you run the SSO service yourself
Lower long-term TCO for predictable B2B SSO workloads
what_to_export: User profiles (email, name, metadata) via Auth0 user export
Password hashes (bcrypt) — request via Auth0 support if needed
Social/identity provider connections and their client IDs
Enterprise SAML connection metadata (entityID, certs, ACS URLs)
MFA enrollments (note: most factors require user re-enrollment)
export_method: Use Auth0's Management API and the Import/Export extension (or User Import/Export job) to pull users and metadata as JSON. For password hashes specifically, follow Auth0's documented hash export flow (typically via support ticket). Export SAML/SCIM connection metadata from the Auth0 dashboard. Consult Auth0's official migration documentation for exact endpoints and any current rate limits.
import_method: Stand up BoxyHQ Jackson (the SAML/OIDC service) via Docker or their Helm chart, configure your database, then load tenants and SAML connections through Jackson's admin API. For end-user accounts, you'll typically pair Jackson with your own user store or SaaS Starter Kit — Jackson itself focuses on the SSO bridge rather than full user management. Re-create SCIM directories per tenant. Validate end-to-end SSO with one tenant before cutover.

concept_mapping.note: Multi-tenant model maps cleanly; review tenant identifier conventions in Jackson docs
Re-upload IdP metadata XML per connection
Provisioning endpoints differ — re-issue tokens to IdPs
Jackson does not run server-side hooks the same way; port logic into your backend
BoxyHQ Jackson handles the SAML/OIDC handshake; the login UI is yours

concept_mapping.target: Your application DB / SaaS Starter Kit
concept_mapping.note: Jackson is not a primary user store — plan persistence separately

gotchas: BoxyHQ Jackson focuses on SAML/OIDC SSO — you still need a user database and password flow elsewhere
Password hash imports require Auth0 to release them; not all hash algorithms are universally portable
MFA factors (TOTP, WebAuthn) typically require user re-enrollment after migration
Self-hosting means you own uptime, key rotation, and database backups
Test SCIM provisioning per IdP — Okta, Entra ID, and Google each have quirks
Audit log format and retention windows differ; check your compliance requirements

email.subject: Migrating from Auth0 to BoxyHQ — kickoff plan
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to move our auth stack from Auth0 to BoxyHQ. The main drivers are predictable cost (no MAU billing) and keeping user data in our infrastructure.

Proposed phases:
1. Stand up BoxyHQ Jackson in staging and wire one pilot tenant.
2. Export users, SAML connections, and SCIM config from Auth0.
3. Re-issue IdP metadata to enterprise customers and validate SSO end-to-end.
4. Cut over per-tenant with a rollback plan.

Rough effort: 1-3 weeks. Open questions: password hash export format, MFA re-enrollment policy, and which Jackson deployment topology we want. Will share a detailed runbook once staging is up.

Thanks
